name: SQLFluff
pricing_notes: Fully open source (MIT); no hosted service

auth.notes: CLI tool and Python library; no auth required

performance.notes: Linting small files <500ms; large SQL files or many rules can take 1-5s; CLI batch mode efficient

agent_readiness.notes: SQL linter and auto-formatter with dbt template support. Configurable rules (200+). Supports BigQuery, Snowflake, Postgres, MySQL, T-SQL, SparkSQL, Redshift, and more. CLI with --fix mode for auto-formatting. Python API for programmatic lint results. Pre-commit hook integration. Essential for SQL CI/CD quality gates.

gotchas: Slow on large SQL files with many rules enabled
dbt Jinja templating requires dbt templater configured (extra setup)
Some dialect-specific rules not fully implemented
Python API less documented than CLI usage
best_when: SQL code quality gates in CI/CD pipelines
dbt projects needing consistent SQL style enforcement
Agents generating SQL that needs validation before execution
Teams wanting automated SQL formatting (--fix mode)
avoid_when: Runtime query validation (use database EXPLAIN instead)
Non-SQL files (use ESLint, Black, etc. for other languages)
summary: SQLFluff is the leading SQL linter and auto-formatter with 200+ configurable rules and support for 15+ SQL dialects. CLI with --fix mode for automatic formatting. Python API for programmatic lint results. dbt Jinja template support. Pre-commit hook integration. Best for SQL CI/CD quality gates and enforcing consistent SQL style across data engineering teams.
